wizardAlchemyCodes.title: Wizard Alchemy Codes
wizardAlchemyCodes.intro: Redeem launch and event rewards early so your race progression and farming setup starts faster.

cards.name: Active Codes
cards.description: Use WIZARD and RELEASE for Race Rerolls, then save rerolls until you decide your target race.

cards.name: Redeem Steps
cards.description: Open Settings, enter a valid code in the Type Here box, and press Claim in Utilities.

cards.name: Failed Claim Fixes
cards.description: Check exact spelling, rejoin if Claim does not respond, and confirm the code is still active.

cards.name: Usage Priority
cards.description: Claim codes before spending Robux so you can test race outcomes without paid rerolls.

wizardAlchemyBeginnerGuide.title: Wizard Alchemy Beginner Guide
wizardAlchemyBeginnerGuide.intro: Follow this opening route to stabilize early combat, potion unlocks, and gold efficiency.

steps.title: Claim launch rewards first
steps.description: Redeem WIZARD and RELEASE immediately to secure rerolls for race progression.

steps.title: Practice movement and spell controls
steps.description: Get comfortable with dash, attack, and spell keys before you challenge stronger targets.

steps.title: Farm starter materials on Departure Isle
steps.description: Focus Blueberry, Seagull Egg, and Goblin Finger to unlock early potion paths.

steps.title: Brew low-magic spells early
steps.description: Start with Wind Blade, Rock Blast, and Ice Spike for reliable first unlocks.

wizardAlchemyRaceTierList.title: Wizard Alchemy Race Tier List
wizardAlchemyRaceTierList.intro: Use rerolls with clear targets so you avoid wasting free launches on low-impact races.

tiers.name: S Tier Targets
tiers.description: Thestrals for premium DPS scaling and faster combat progression.

tiers.name: A Tier Targets
tiers.description: Stellar Ambassador and Death Eater for stable all-purpose growth.

tiers.name: B Tier Targets
tiers.description: Fiendish Demon and Ice Crystal for element-focused builds.

tiers.name: Early Upgrade
tiers.description: Werewolf is a practical boost over Human while you save rerolls.

wizardAlchemyPotionsGuide.title: Wizard Alchemy Potions Guide
wizardAlchemyPotionsGuide.intro: Potions drive spell access, so your material route should always serve the next unlock tier.

cards.name: Starter Potions
cards.description: Wind Blade, Rock Blast, and Ice Spike are efficient first unlocks.

cards.name: Mid-Game Potions
cards.description: Fire Arrow, Earth Shield, and Ice Turtle support broader combat options.

cards.name: Late Potions
cards.description: Dragon Breath, Lotus Bloom, and Radiant Sword require high Magic thresholds.

cards.name: Refinement Path
cards.description: Use duplicates to refine potion grade and increase spell power and range.

wizardAlchemyMaterialsFarmingGuide.title: Wizard Alchemy Materials Farming Guide
wizardAlchemyMaterialsFarmingGuide.intro: Track core materials by magic power, drop source, and farming priority so every route supports your next potion unlock.

cards.name: Early Gathering Route
cards.description: Start with Blueberry Bushes, Bird Nests, and Mushroom Clusters for fast first-brew ingredients.

cards.name: Goblin and Dwarf Drops
cards.description: Farm Knife Goblins, Archer Goblins, Pickaxe Dwarves, and Warhammer Dwarves based on listed drop rates.

cards.name: Rare Material Targets
cards.description: Push mutant enemies and Dwarf King runs for Flame Crest, Copper Earring, and Furnace Core.

cards.name: Elemental Shard Farming
cards.description: Use chest loops and mutant routes when targeting Ice, Light, Dark, Wind, Fire, and Earth shard drops.

wizardAlchemyWandsGuide.title: Wizard Alchemy Wands Guide
wizardAlchemyWandsGuide.intro: Compare wand cost, attack, and bonus stats before spending gold so each upgrade improves farming speed and combat consistency.

cards.name: Wingbird Wand
cards.description: 200 Gold, 20 Attack, and +5% Attack Bonus make this the first practical upgrade from starter damage.

cards.name: Azure and Demon Trident
cards.description: 500 to 8,000 Gold options that raise attack and bonus scaling for mid-game farming.

cards.name: Ice Star Wand
cards.description: 20,000 Gold with cooldown reduction, bought at the waterfall near the starting village.

cards.name: Ember Staff Wand
cards.description: 30,000 Gold late option with higher attack and crit support for stronger combat bursts.

wizardAlchemyMonstersAndBossDropsGuide.title: Wizard Alchemy Monsters and Boss Drops Guide
wizardAlchemyMonstersAndBossDropsGuide.intro: Use location-based enemy routes to farm gold, potion materials, and rare shard drops with less wasted travel time.

cards.name: Early Forest Targets
cards.description: Pickaxe Dwarf and Knife Goblin routes are efficient for early emblems, fingers, and gold.

cards.name: Beach and Cave Routes
cards.description: Warhammer Dwarf and Archer Goblin loops improve Golden Tooth, Goblin Bone, and Copper Earring farming.

cards.name: Mutant Priority Farm
cards.description: Mutant Warhammer Dwarf and Mutant Goblin Archer are top targets for shards and rare material drops.

cards.name: Dwarf King Boss Runs
cards.description: Use Dwarf King rotations when targeting Flame Crest, Copper Earring, and Furnace Core.

wizardAlchemyEquipmentGuide.title: Wizard Alchemy Equipment Guide
wizardAlchemyEquipmentGuide.intro: Plan robe and wizard hat purchases by HP value so your survivability keeps pace with wand and potion progression.

cards.name: Early Robe Value
cards.description: Starmoon Robe and Golden Reverie Robe are efficient first HP upgrades from Roger's shop.

cards.name: Mid Robe Upgrade
cards.description: Starlight Robe is a higher-cost option once your gold farming route is stable.

cards.name: Wizard Hat Choices
cards.description: Starmoon, Golden Reverie, and Starlight hats add extra HP for safer monster and boss loops.

cards.name: Recommended Buy Order
cards.description: Secure your first wand, then add robe and hat HP upgrades before expensive late-game weapon jumps.

faq.title: Wizard Alchemy
faq.subtitle: Common questions for Wizard Alchemy progression, rewards, and platform access

questions.question: How do Wizard Alchemy codes work?
questions.answer: Wizard Alchemy codes are redeemed in Settings and usually grant Race Rerolls or event rewards.

questions.question: What should I do first in Wizard Alchemy?
questions.answer: Claim codes, farm starter materials, unlock low-magic potions, and secure basic wand plus HP upgrades.

questions.question: Which race should I target in Wizard Alchemy?
questions.answer: Werewolf is a practical early target, while Thestrals is a top-end damage target when rerolls allow.

questions.question: How do I progress potions in Wizard Alchemy?
questions.answer: Farm materials by drop source, brew unlock potions in order, and refine duplicates for stronger outcomes.

questions.question: Where can I play Wizard Alchemy?
questions.answer: Wizard Alchemy is available on Roblox through its official game page.

questions.question: How can I get Wizard Alchemy updates quickly?
questions.answer: Track Roblox game updates and the official community channels for code and progression changes.
